blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلْمُرْسَلَـٰتِ عُرْفًا (۱)
By the [angelic] emissaries sent successively, (1)
فَٱلْعَـٰصِفَـٰتِ عَصْفًا (۲)
by those who sweep along like gale, (2)
وَٱلنَّـٰشِرَٰتِ نَشْرًا (۳)
by those who publish [the Divine messages] far and wide, (3)
فَٱلْفَـٰرِقَـٰتِ فَرْقًا (٤)
by those who separate [the truth from falsehood] distinctly, (4)
فَٱلْمُلْقِيَـٰتِ ذِكْرًا (٥)
by those who inspire [God’s] remembrance, (5)
عُذْرًا أَوْ نُذْرًا (٦)
as exemption or warning: (6)
إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَوَٰقِعٌ (۷)
what you are promised will surely befall. (7)

Key Points

The word ‘urf (عُرف) means continuous or successive. The mane of a horse, which has rows of hair, is called ‘urf. This word also means good and praiseworthy, and the word ma‘ruf (good deed) is derived from it. Three interpretations have been presented for the matters sworn upon in the first five verses of this Surah—namely, Al-Mursalāt, Al-‘Āṣifāt, An-Nāshirāt, Al-Fāriqāt, and Al-Mulqiyāt: A: Some commentators believe that they refer to various types of winds and storms; (247) B: Some believe that they refer to various types of angels; (248) C: Some believe that the first and second verses relate to wind and storm, and the third through fifth verses relate to angels. This distinction is due to the type of conjunction used, as the letter wāw (و) at the beginning of a verse indicates separation, and the letter fā’ (ف) at the beginning of a verse indicates connection.

Al-‘Āṣifāt (العاصفات) is the plural of ‘āṣif (عاصف), meaning a destructive and severe wind, as we read in verse 12 of Surah Yunus: «ريح عاصف» (a violent wind). The word ‘aṣf (عصف) means a strong gale, such as: «كعصفٍ مأكول» (like straw eaten up) (249), and «والحبّ ذو العصف» (and the grain having husks) (250).

If we consider Al-‘Āṣifāt to refer to angels, the meaning of the verse is that the angels execute the Divine command with intensity and speed. Similar to the verses that state: «يفعلون ما يؤمرون» (they do what they are commanded) (251) or «هم بأمره يعملون» (they act by His command) (252).

The juxtaposition of oaths sworn upon winds and angels might be due to the similarity between wind and angel, in that both are subtle and swift, and both play a role in creation and the management of affairs.

The word Mulqiyāt (ملقیات) is used in the plural form, perhaps because sometimes other angels descend accompanying the Angel of Revelation, Gabriel, or perhaps because sometimes an angel, and sometimes other individuals or matters, become a means of reminding man and conveying issues to him.

Almighty Allah must complete the proof (Hujjah) against people and prevent excuses and pretexts. «عذراً» (as an excuse). We also read in verse 134 of Surah Taha: If We had destroyed them before the coming of the prophets, they would have offered an excuse, saying: Why did You not send a prophet before this so that we might follow him and not be afflicted?

There is a natural progression in the three attributes: Nāshirāt, Fāriqāt, and Mulqiyāt. That is, first, angels must spread knowledge: «الناشرات» (the dispersers); then, in its light, truth and falsehood, and lawful and unlawful, are separated: «الفارقات» (the separators); and following that, the reminder (Dhikr) is conveyed. «فالملقيات ذكراً» (then those who cast the remembrance) (253).

247) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi. 248) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 249) Al-Fil, 5. 250) Ar-Raḥmān, 12. 251) An-Naḥl, 50. 252) Al-Anbiyā’, 27. 253) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Whatever is sent forth by Allah is sacred and worthy of being sworn upon. «والمرسلات»

2- Allah’s favors are perpetual and continuous. (254) «والمرسلات عرفا»

3- Whatever descends from Allah is good. (255) «والمرسلات عرفا»

4- In the system of creation, even the blowing of the wind is not accidental; rather, the Creator of the universe sends it forth. «المرسلات»

5- Important missions must be carried out swiftly. «عصفاً»

6- In the Divine worldview, gentleness and severity are each valuable in their proper place. «عرفاً... عصفاً»

7- Development and expansion do not mean ignoring principles and frameworks. «والناشرات نشراً فالفارقات فرقاً»

8- In the system of existence, tasks are divided, and each task is entrusted to an individual or a group; this is the secret to successful management. «المرسلات، الناشرات، الفارقات»

9- In the Divine system, creation (takwīn) and legislation (tashrī‘) are side by side. «والمرسلات... فالملقيات ذكراً»

10- In guidance missions, the message must be made understandable to the audience. «فالملقيات ذكراً»

11- Every reminder (dhikr), inspiration, knowledge, and righteous thought comes from Allah. «فالملقيات ذكراً»

12- By establishing distinctions between truth and falsehood, Allah has both completed the proof: «عذراً» (as an excuse), and He gives warning. «نُذراً» (or a warning).

13- In guidance missions, the path of excuse-making must be blocked. «عذراً أو نذراً»

14- For important matters, such as the occurrence of the Resurrection, multiple oaths are beneficial and illuminating. «والمرسلات، فالعاصفات، والناشرات، فالفارقات، فالملقيات، إنّما توعدون لواقع»

15- Humanity constantly needs admonition and remembrance regarding the Resurrection. «فالملقيات ذكراً... إنّما توعدون لواقع»

254) This meaning is derived if we consider the word ‘urf (عُرف) to mean successive or continuous.

255) If we consider the word ‘urf (عُرف) to mean goodness and virtue, similar to the verse «وأمر بالعرف» (and enjoin what is right).

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Mursalat

This Surah has fifty verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse, which begins with Al-Mursalāt, referring to the angels who are sent forth by Allah to carry out various tasks.

This Surah, like Surahs Al-Anbiyā’ (234), Al-Qamar (235), Al-Hajj (236), Al-Wāqi‘ah (237), Al-Hāqqah (238), Al-Qiyāmah (239), An-Naba’ (240), At-Takwīr (241), Al-Infiṭār (242), Al-Inshiqāq (243), Al-Ghāshiyah (244), Al-Qāri‘ah (245), and Az-Zalzalah (246), begins with verses related to the Resurrection (Ma‘ād). Just as it begins with an oath upon angels, similar to Surahs Fāṭir, Adh-Dhāriyāt, and An-Nāzi‘āt.

In this Surah, the verse «ويل يومئذ للمكذّبين» (Woe, that Day, to the deniers) is repeated ten times, just as the verse «فبأيّ آلاء ربّكما تكذّبان» (Then which of the favors of your Lord will you deny?) is repeated in Surah Ar-Raḥmān, and the verse «فكيف كان عذابي ونذر» (Then how was My punishment and My warnings?) is repeated in Surah Al-Qamar.

234) «اقترب للناس حسابهم». 235) «اقتربت الساعة». 236) «إنّ زلزلة الساعة شى ء عظيم». 237) «إذا وقعت الواقعة». 238) «الحاقّة ما الحاقّة». 239) «لا أقسم بيوم القيامة». 240) «عمّ يتساءلون». 241) «إذا الشمس كوّرت». 242) «إذا السماء انفطرت». 243) «إذا السماء انشقّت». 244) «هل أتاك حديث الغاشية». 245) «القارعة ما القارعة». 246) «إذا زلزلت الأرض زلزالها».

Ayahs

فَإِذَا ٱلنُّجُومُ طُمِسَتْ (۸)
When the stars are blotted out (8)
وَإِذَا ٱلسَّمَآءُ فُرِجَتْ (۹)
and the sky is cleft, (9)
وَإِذَا ٱلْجِبَالُ نُسِفَتْ (۱٠)
when the mountains are scattered [like dust] (10)
وَإِذَا ٱلرُّسُلُ أُقِّتَتْ (۱۱)
and time is set for the apostles [to bear witness] (11)
لِأَىِّ يَوْمٍ أُجِّلَتْ (۱۲)
—for what day has [all] that been set [to occur]? (12)
لِيَوْمِ ٱلْفَصْلِ (۱۳)
For the Day of Judgement! (13)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا يَوْمُ ٱلْفَصْلِ (۱٤)
And what will show you what is the Day of Judgement!? (14)

Key Points

Ṭams (طَمس) means vanishing and being obliterated, and perhaps the intention is the vanishing of their light. Faraj (فرج) means a rift or split, and "و اذا السماء فرجت" means the sky splitting open and tearing apart. Uqqitat (اقّتت) is derived from waqt (وقت) meaning the setting of a time for the prophets to bear witness on the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah), which has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. As we read in Surah Al-Ma'idah: "یوم یجمع اللّه الرسل فیقول ماذا اجتبم" (256) The Day when Allah gathers all the Messengers, then He will say: How did your nation respond to your call? One of the names of Qiyamah is Yawm al-Faṣl (یوم الفصل), meaning the Day of Separation. We read in the Quran: "انّ اللّه یفصل بینهم یوم القیامة" (257) Almighty Allah will separate between them (and that to which they were attached) on the Day of Qiyamah. In these verses, the greatness of Qiyamah is mentioned using three expressions: "لاىّ یوم اجّلت لیوم الفصل و ما ادراك یوم الفصل" and the repetition of the word Yawm (Day) is a sign of the greatness of that Day. Similar verses to these are also seen in other Surahs of the Quran: "فاذا النجوم طمست" (When the stars are obliterated), in another place He says: "و اذا النّجوم انكدرت" (258) (And when the stars fall, losing their luster). "واذا السماء فرجت" (And when the sky is split open), in another place He says: "اذا السّماء انشقت" (259) (When the heaven is split asunder). "واذا الجبال نسفت" (And when the mountains are scattered), in another place He says: "ویسئلونك عن الجبال فقل ینسفها ربّى نسفا" (260) (And they ask you concerning the mountains, say: My Lord will scatter them utterly). "واذا الرّسل اقّتت" (And when the Messengers are gathered for their appointed time), in another place He says: "یوم یجمع اللّه الرّسل" (261) (The Day when Allah gathers the Messengers).

256) Al-Ma'idah, 109. 257) Al-Hajj, 17. 258) At-Takwir, 2. 259) Al-Inshiqaq, 1. 260) Taha, 105. 261) Al-Ma'idah, 109.

Messages

1- The current order of creation will be transformed on the Day of Qiyamah; the stars will vanish and the sky will split open, and this transformation has been recalled repeatedly in the Quran: "فاذا النجوم طمست و اذا السماء فرجت"

2- The reality of Qiyamah can only be known through revelation. "و ما ادراك ما یوم الفصل"

3- On the Day of Qiyamah, a specific time has been set for the prophets to bear witness against their nations. "و اذا الرّسل اقّتت، لاىّ یوم اجّلت"

Ayahs

وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۱٥)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (15)
أَلَمْ نُهْلِكِ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۱٦)
Did We not destroy the former generations, (16)
ثُمَّ نُتْبِعُهُمُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِينَ (۱۷)
[and] then made the latter ones follow them? (17)
كَذَٰلِكَ نَفْعَلُ بِٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۱۸)
That is how We deal with the guilty. (18)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۱۹)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (19)

Key Points

In this Surah, the Ayah, «ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین» is repeated many times, and this warning appears at the end of every few verses. The word «ویل» is used in the context of warning and threatening with punishment and destruction, and based on narrations, it is the name of a specific valley in Hell or the name of one of its gates.

Messages

1- Deniers of religion are exposed to destruction and annihilation, and Allah's wrath upon them is a Divine tradition (Sunnah). «ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین ألم نهلك الاولین»

2- Awaken dormant consciences by presenting the history of those who passed away. «ألم نهلك الاولین»

3- Denying religion has a long history. «المكذّبین... الاوّلین»

4- Human civilizations, if they cross the boundary of religion, face decline and fall, giving way to other civilizations. «نهلك الاوّلین... نتبعهم الآخرین»

5- The root of crime and corruption in society is the denial of religion and religious values. «المكذّبین... المجرمین»

6- Allah's wrath is governed by law and encompasses only the sinners and the criminals. «كذلك نفعل بالمجرمین»

7- Divine punishments are not based on vengeance, but rather on the deservingness of human beings. «كذلك نفعل بالمجرمین»

Ayahs

أَلَمْ نَخْلُقكُّم مِّن مَّآءٍ مَّهِينٍ (۲٠)
Have We not created you from a base fluid, (20)
فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ فِى قَرَارٍ مَّكِينٍ (۲۱)
[and] then lodged it in a secure abode (21)
إِلَىٰ قَدَرٍ مَّعْلُومٍ (۲۲)
until a known span [of time]? (22)
فَقَدَرْنَا فَنِعْمَ ٱلْقَـٰدِرُونَ (۲۳)
Then We determined; and how excellent determiners We are! (23)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۲٤)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (24)

Key Points

"Mahīn" (مهین) means insignificant, weak, and little, and "Makīn" (مكین) means firm and stable. For the production of a product, certain requirements and conditions are needed, including: defining goals, securing materials, planning and programming, scheduling implementation stages, post-production support services, repair, and correction. In the creation of man, all these stages have been foreseen and executed: Goal: Man's voluntary and spiritual perfection. «و ما خلقت الجنّ و الانس الاّ لیعبدون» (262) Raw materials: dust (earth) and sperm/semen. «من ماء مهین», Place of production: «فى قرار مكین», Production plan and program: «نطفة، علقة، مضغة،...», Scheduling: «الى قدر معلوم», Internal programming: «فالهمها فجورها و تقواها» (263), Along with external control: «انّا هدیناه السبیل» (264) and «انّ علینا للهدى» (265). Post-production services: «نحن قسمنا بینهم معیشتهم» (266). Repair and correction: «یقبل التوبة» (267), «انّه غفور رحیم» (268). The masterpiece of creation is that Allah designed the creation of man, with all its complexity, upon a drop of water, which no artist has the ability to design upon. «نخلقكم من ماء مهین» Allah, in the creation of man, once awarded Himself the title of «احسن الخالقین» (269) and here He presents the title «نعم القادرون». Yes, He is the best Creator and the best Possessor of Power. The phrase «نعم القادرون» can be interpreted in two ways: A. Considering the phrases «الى قدر» and «فقدّرنا» which preceded it, the intent of «قادرون» may be measurement (or estimation), meaning that Allah has applied the best measurement regarding man. B. Considering that the discussion is about creation and its stages, which require power, the intent of «قادرون» may be power and ability, meaning that Allah has employed perfect power in the creation of man. Allah is the best in everything: The best Helper and the best Guardian: «نعم المولى و نعم النصیر» (270) The best Trustee: «نعم الوكیل» (271) The best Spreader of the Cradle (Earth): «فنعم الماهدون» (272) The best Responder: «فنعم المجیبون» (273) The best Rewarder: «نعم اجرالعاملین» (274) The best Possessor of Power and Determiner: «نعم القادرون»

(262) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 56. (263) Ash-Shams, 8. (264) Al-Insān, 3. (265) Al-Layl, 12. (266) Az-Zukhruf, 32. (267) At-Tawbah, 104. (268) Al-An'ām, 54. (269) Al-Mu’minūn, 14. (270) Al-Anfāl, 40. (271) Āl ‘Imrān, 173. (272) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 48. (273) As-Sāffāt, 75. (274) Āl ‘Imrān, 136.

Messages

1- The creation of man from insignificant water is both a sign of Divine greatness and power, and proof of the Resurrection and the recreation of man. «ألم نخلقكم من ماء مهین»

2- Remembering past weaknesses and insignificance eliminates human pride and arrogance. «ألم نخلقكم من ماء مهین»

3- In the method of invitation (Da'wah) and propagation, speak about tangible matters. «ألم نخلقكم من ماء مهین»

4- Let us learn from Allah and seek stability and firmness in work and production. «فى قرار مكین»

5- Determination (Divine Decree) and scheduling are among the principles of Divine creation. «الى قدر معلوم فقدّرنا»

6- Fetal growth is based on precise Divine measurements. «فقدرنا فنعم القادرون»

Ayahs

أَلَمْ نَجْعَلِ ٱلْأَرْضَ كِفَاتًا (۲٥)
Have We not made the earth a receptacle (25)
أَحْيَآءً وَأَمْوَٰتًا (۲٦)
for the living and the dead, (26)
وَجَعَلْنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِىَ شَـٰمِخَـٰتٍ وَأَسْقَيْنَـٰكُم مَّآءً فُرَاتًا (۲۷)
and set in it lofty [and] firm mountains, and given you agreeable water to drink? (27)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۲۸)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (28)
ٱنطَلِقُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ مَا كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ (۲۹)
[The faithless will be told:] ‘Proceed toward what you used to deny! (29)
ٱنطَلِقُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ ظِلٍّ ذِى ثَلَـٰثِ شُعَبٍ (۳٠)
Proceed toward the triple-forked shadow, (30)
لَّا ظَلِيلٍ وَلَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱللَّهَبِ (۳۱)
neither shady nor of any avail against the flames. (31)
إِنَّهَا تَرْمِى بِشَرَرٍ كَٱلْقَصْرِ (۳۲)
Indeed it throws up [giant] sparks like castles, (32)
كَأَنَّهُۥ جِمَـٰلَتٌ صُفْرٌ (۳۳)
[bright] like yellow camels. (33)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۳٤)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (34)

Key Points

"Kifāt" (كفات) is the name of a place where something is deposited and gathered. We read in narrations that Imam Sadiq عليه السلام looked at the graveyard and said: This is the kifāt of the dead, and He looked at the houses and said: This is the kifāt of the living. (275)

The meaning of ẓill dhī thalāth shu‘ab (ظلّ ذى ثلاث شعب) is a shadow created by the massive smoke of Hellfire, but it is clear that this shadow is not only not comforting but is burning and painful. This smoke is so dense and massive that it branches out: a branch from above the head, a branch from the right side, and a branch from the left side, enveloping the person in Hell and swallowing him.

The flames that rise from the Hellfire are like tall worldly palaces and like yellow camels. It is as if the Quran is threatening the palace dwellers and the hoarders of wealth, warning them to beware of being caught by the palace-like sparks of Hell.

Shāmikhāt (شامخات) means tall mountains, and furāt (فرات) means pleasant/sweet water. Ẓill ẓalīl (ظلّ ظلیل) means permanent shade, and ẓill lā ẓalīl (ظلّ لا ظلیل) means useless and non-cooling shade. Jimālat (جمالت) is the plural of jamal (جمل), meaning camel, and ṣufr (صُفر) is the plural of aṣfar (اصفر), meaning yellow.

Allah created the Earth in the most suitable form in terms of its vastness, its movement, the provision of security through mountains, and the provision of necessary water for humans, plants, and animals. He made the soil the best means for eliminating waste, polluted water, and burying the dead. He placed within it a type of purification plant with germ-killing properties, such that the Earth takes in polluted water and delivers clear water. This purification plant is natural, free, permanent, universal, and never suffers a technical fault. Truly, if the Earth did not exist, bad smells and putrefaction would bother everyone.

Divine wrath surrounds the criminals from all sides, such that there is no way to escape, and the only path open is toward Hell. Inṭaliqū ilā mā kuntum bihi tukadhdhibūn (Go forth to that which you used to deny), just as He says in other verses: Kullamā arādū an yakhrujū minhā min ghammin u‘īdū fīhā wa dhūqū ‘adhāba al-ḥarīq (Every time they wish to get out of it because of anguish, they will be returned to it, and [it will be said], "Taste the punishment of the burning fire.") (276), Aḥāṭa bihim surādiqūhā (Its enclosure will encompass them.) (277)

275) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 276) Al-Hajj, 22. 277) Al-Kahf, 29.

Messages

1- The surface of the Earth is the best place for the living, and its interior is the best place for the dead. Alam naj‘ali al-arḍa kifātan aḥyā’an wa amwātan

2- Mountains are sources for providing the necessary water. Rawāsiya shāmikhātin wa asqaynākum mā’an

3- The scene of the Day of Judgment is so harsh and difficult that it is as if going to Hell is a kind of release for the criminals. Inṭaliqū ilā...

4- Hell is surrounded by thick smoke that creates a shadow, but there is no comfort in it. Ẓill... lā ẓalīl

5- Palaces and wealth unjustly acquired in this world will turn into the sparks of Hell in the Hereafter. Kal-qaṣri ka-annahu jimālatun ṣufr

6- If the flames of Hellfire are like a tall palace and like a massive camel, what will the Fire itself be like! Bi-shararin kal-qaṣri ka-annahu jimālatun ṣufr

7- The root of all crimes is corrupt belief. Because whoever lacks faith and denies the truth will commit every kind of transgression. Waylun yawma’idhin lil-mukadhdhibīn

Ayahs

هَـٰذَا يَوْمُ لَا يَنطِقُونَ (۳٥)
This is a day wherein they will not speak, (35)
وَلَا يُؤْذَنُ لَهُمْ فَيَعْتَذِرُونَ (۳٦)
nor will they be permitted to offer excuses. (36)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۳۷)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (37)
هَـٰذَا يَوْمُ ٱلْفَصْلِ ۖ جَمَعْنَـٰكُمْ وَٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (۳۸)
‘This is the Day of Judgement. We have brought you together with the former generations. (38)
فَإِن كَانَ لَكُمْ كَيْدٌ فَكِيدُونِ (۳۹)
If you have any stratagems [left], try them out against Me!’ (39)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (٤٠)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (40)

Key Points

The Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) has multiple stations (or stages). In one station, lips will be sealed, and no one will have the ability to speak: «هذا یوم لاینطقون» (This is a Day wherein they speak not) and «الیوم نختم على افواههم» (Today We seal over their mouths) (278). But in other stations, there will be conversations. Many verses in the Quran describe the conversations and lamentations of the criminals on the Day of Judgment. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah is too just to prevent His servant from presenting an excuse if he has one, but since the sinful servant has no excuse, he will not be permitted to speak in the Divine Court (279). In this world (Dunya), man is able to deceive himself or others, and through wealth, power, deception, or by means of promises, threats, bribery, or familial/relational ties, he can devise a solution for himself. But on the Day of Judgment, the path for all these actions is closed. «فان كان لكم كید فكیدون» (So if you have a plot, then plot against Me).

278) Yasin, 65. 279) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 178.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, criminals will not speak either out of fear, or because it is useless, or because their mouths are sealed, or because all matters are clear. «هذا یوم لاینطقون»

2- The preacher and educator should not tire of repeating warnings. In this Surah, the verse «ویلٌ یومئذ للمكذبین» (Woe, that Day, to the deniers) is repeated ten times.

3- The Day of Judgment is both the Day of Gathering (Jam') and the Day of Separation/Decision (Fasl). «هذا یوم الفصل جمعناكم» (This is the Day of Separation; We have gathered you). (On the one hand, everyone is gathered, and no one is left behind: «و حشرناهم فلم نغادر منهم احداً» (and We gathered them, and did not leave behind anyone of them) (280); and on the other hand, a decision is made between the good and the bad, and separation occurs; even the inhabitants of Paradise have ranks within Paradise.)

4- The Day of Judgment is a day of reckoning and judgment, not a day for making excuses. «لایؤذن لهم فیعتذرون... هذا یوم الفصل»

5- On the Day of Judgment, there is no escape, no deception, no ransom is accepted, and there is no path to salvation. «ان كان لكم كید فكیدون»

6- On the Day of Judgment, criminals are powerless and helpless regarding any kind of solution or strategy. «فان كان لكم كید فكیدون»

280) Kahf, 47.

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى ظِلَـٰلٍ وَعُيُونٍ (٤۱)
Indeed the Godwary will be amid shades and springs (41)
وَفَوَٰكِهَ مِمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ (٤۲)
and [enjoying] such fruits as they desire. (42)
كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ هَنِيٓـًٔۢا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (٤۳)
[They will be told:] ‘Enjoy your food and drink, [a reward] for what you used to do. (43)
إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (٤٤)
Thus do We reward the virtuous.’ (44)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (٤٥)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (45)

Key Points

In the previous verses, Allah described the characteristics of Hellfire, including the smoke and the three-pronged fire that surrounds it. In contrast, this verse mentions three blessings for the people of piety (Taqwa): "ظلال و عیون و فواكه" (shades, springs, and fruits).

The word "هنیئاً" (pleasantly/wholesomely) is used where there is no hardship or unpleasantness following the enjoyment, and it is completely palatable and agreeable.

One of the educational methods of the Quran is the method of comparison:

Reward of the Pious: Punishment of the Criminals:
«فى ظلال و عیون» «ظلّ ذى ثلاث شعب لاظلیل»
«كلوا واشربوا هنیئاً» «كلوا وتمتّعوا قلیلاً انّكم مجرمون»
«انّا كذلك نجزى المحسنین» «كذلك نفعل بالمجرمین»
«هنیئاً بما كنتم تعملون» «انطلقوا الى ما كنتم به تكذبون»

Question: Why does the verse "ویل یومئذ للمكذبین" (Woe, that Day, to the deniers) also appear after the verses related to Paradise? Answer: Perhaps because the regret of being deprived of the blessings of Paradise is itself the greatest of torments.

Messages

  1. Paradise and Hellfire have different types of shade. «ظلّ ذى ثلاث شعب...فى ظلال و عیون»

  2. Humans seek variety, and in Paradise, the gardens, springs, fruits, foods, and rivers are all diverse. «فى ظلال و عیون و فواكه ممّا یشتهون»

  3. The presence of water and trees together is a sign of complete fulfillment. «فى ظلال و عیون»

  4. The deprivations of the believers in this world will be compensated by fulfillment in the Hereafter. «انّ المتقین فى ظلال و عیون»

  5. The Resurrection is physical, and its blessings are commensurate with the physical needs of humanity. «و فواكه ممّا یشتهون كلوا واشربوا»

  6. Receiving blessings depends on human performance. «هنیئاً بما كنتم تعملون»

  7. The foods of Paradise do not entail any side effects or hardship. «كلوا واشربوا هنیئاً»

  8. Paradise is given for a price (effort), not an excuse. «بما كنتم تعملون»

  9. An action is valuable if it is continuous and enduring, not seasonal or temporary. «كنتم تعملون»

  10. The prerequisite for piety (Taqwa) is effort and goodness (Ihsan). «المتّقین... كنتم تعملون... المحسنین»

  11. The Divine tradition (Sunnah) regarding the punishment and reward of humans is consistent. «انّا كذلك نجزى المحسنین»

  12. The repetition of the warning is a sign of Allah's grace, so that perhaps the arrogant may come to their senses and cease denying the truths of the religion. «ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین»

Ayahs

كُلُوا۟ وَتَمَتَّعُوا۟ قَلِيلًا إِنَّكُم مُّجْرِمُونَ (٤٦)
‘Eat and enjoy a little! You are indeed guilty.’ (46)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (٤۷)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (47)
وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱرْكَعُوا۟ لَا يَرْكَعُونَ (٤۸)
When they are told, ‘Bow down [in prayer],’ they do not bow down! (48)
وَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (٤۹)
Woe to the deniers on that day! (49)
فَبِأَىِّ حَدِيثٍۭ بَعْدَهُۥ يُؤْمِنُونَ (٥٠)
So what discourse will they believe after this? (50)

Key Points

A physician allows a patient to eat any food in two situations: first, when the patient has been cured and is healthy; and second, when the individual's illness is incurable and untreatable, and the physician has despaired of his recovery. Similarly, in the Quran, the command “Kulū” (Eat) is sometimes a sign of hope and health, and sometimes a sign of Divine wrath, as Allah addresses the criminals in the verses, saying: «كلوا و تمتّعوا قلیلاً انكم مجرمون» (Eat and enjoy yourselves for a little while; indeed, you are criminals) and similar to the verse «اعملوا ما شئتم»(281) (Do whatever you wish).

The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله commanded the tribe of Thaqif to pray. They said: We will not bow or prostrate before anyone. The Prophet said: There is no good in a religion that does not contain bowing (rukūʿ) and prostration (sujūd). «لا خیر فى دین لیس فیه ركوع و لاسجود»(282)

After the Conquest of Mecca, the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله asked the wife of Abu Sufyan (Mu'awiyah's mother): How do you view Islam? She said: Islam is good except for three things: hijab (veiling), the adhan (call to prayer) of Bilal, and bowing (rukūʿ) and prostration (sujūd). The Prophet replied: As for hijab, it is the best covering; prayer without rukūʿ and sujūd is not prayer; and Bilal is the best servant.(283)

“Hadīth” means new and fresh speech that has no precedent from others.

After rebuilding the Ka'bah, Prophet Abraham asked Allah to provide sustenance only to the people of faith. «و ارزق اهله من الثمرات من آمن منهم» (And provide its people with fruits—those among them who believe). But Allah said: I provide sustenance to the disbelievers (kuffār) in this world as well, and the reckoning of the Day of Judgment is separate from this world. «و من كفر فامتعه...»(284)

Rukūʿ (bowing) is one of the pillars of prayer and its specific characteristic and sign, because sujūd (prostration) can occur independently and outside of prayer, such as the prostration of thanks (sajdat al-shukr). However, rukūʿ has no place other than prayer, and thus the word rak'ah (unit of prayer) is used concerning prayer. «اذا قیل لهم اركعوا لایركعون»

281) Fussilat, 40. 282) Bihar, Vol. 17, p. 52. 283) Tafsir al-Kashif. 284) Al-Baqarah, 126.

Messages

1- Worldly enjoyment is very insignificant compared to the enjoyment of the Hereafter. «كلوا وتمتّعوا قلیلاً»

2- Enjoyment of worldly blessings is not a sign of popularity with Allah. (Criminals also benefit.) «كلوا وتمتّعوا قلیلاً انّكم مجرمون»

3- The benefit criminals derive from Divine blessings is only material benefit. «كلوا وتمتّعوا... انّكم مجرمون»

4- Worldly life, no matter how long, is insignificant compared to the Hereafter. «تمتّعوا قلیلاً»

5- One of the signs of practically denying religion is abandoning prayer. (The abandonment of prayer is placed between the two phrases «مكذّبین» [deniers].) «ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین اذا قیل لهم اركعوا لایركعون ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین»

6- Denying religion and committing crime are inseparable. «انّكم مجرمون ویل یومئذ للمكذّبین»

7- The Quran is the most eloquent, newest, and most worthy message for people. «فباىّ حدیث»

8- The contents of the Quran never become old; they are new and fresh in every era. «فبّاى حدیث بعده»

9- If someone does not believe in the Quran, in which speech will he believe? «فباىّ حدیث بعده یؤمنون»
